entry.text: A depression in the marsh floor is wider than it should be, ringed by dead reeds that lean inward like penitents. The black water at its center barely ripples, but something pale and angular breaks the surface — the corner of a metal crate, sealed and promising. The footing around the rim is treacherous.

choices.label: Wade in along the firm eastern bank
Probe the mud for a safe path with your staff

rim.text: At the rim you can reach the crate's hasp. The mud seals it with a low sucking groan when you pull. Inside, wrapped in oilcloth gone brittle, are rations and a roll of water-purification tablets — someone cached this deliberately, then never came back. The sinkhole trembles once beneath you.

choices.label: Dig deeper into the crate for more
Back off with what you have

deep_cache.text: Beneath the oilcloth you find a second layer: a sealed medical pouch and three scrap-metal ingots bound with wire. You haul them free and back-step to solid ground, legs coated to the knee in grey slick. The sinkhole closes another inch, swallowing the empty crate without a sound.

sudden_sink.text: The bank gives way without warning and you drop thigh-deep into clinging mud. Wrenching free tears something in your hip and costs you precious minutes.

reed_tangle.text: Your staff snaps a submerged root cluster and the rebound rakes a jagged stump across your shin, opening a long shallow cut.

collapse.text: The rim caves inward and you go under to the waist. You drag yourself out, soaked and exhausted, losing most of your haul to the mud.

mire_leg.text: A slow step onto apparently solid ground plunges your leg into concealed mire up to the knee. You wrench free but your boot stays behind.
